rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Ritchie Commercial And Mark Ritchies Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 16, CROSS DEFENDANT RITCHIE COMMERCIAL, MARK RITCHIE'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination. The motion is denied. First, there is little point in this motion because Mr. O'Neil seeks statutory indemnity and a finding of good faith per CCP 877.6 will not bar that claim. See Kantor v. Housing Authority of Fresno County, (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 424. Second, the moving parties do not present substantial evidence that roughly approximates plaintiff's total recovery or moving parties' proportionate liability in compliance with Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: DOUBLE E.D. CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. CARRIE HSIEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Ccp 998 Offer To Compromise And Stipulated Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT CARRIE HSIEH'S Motion To Set Aside CCP 998 Offer To Compromise And Stipulated Judgment. Plaintiff's motion to set aside CCP 998 offer and stipulated judgment is denied. Under CCP § 473(b) a court may, "upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Here, the court finds there was no mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect in Defendant's decision to agree to Plaintiff's 998 settlement offer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DANEAL HARRIS VS. RONNIE BELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS RONNIE BELL AND MONICA BELL'S Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued on the Court's own motion to August 14, 2015, at 9:00 AM in Department 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RYAN VS. SAFEWAY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 17, PLAINTIFF MARY RYAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Amended Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production (part 1 of 2). Granted in part as follows: Request No. 4 seeks "copies of all reports of slip(s), and/or trip(s) and or fall(s) or other incidents at the [store] for the five-year [period] prior to the date of [Plaintiff's fall] and during the one-year period subsequent to the date of [Plaintiff's fall]." The request seeks relevant and probative evidence but is overbroad as to scope and time. Defendant is ordered to produce all non-privileged responsive documents, to the extent such exist, that pertain to slips, trips, or falls taking place in those interior parts of the store to which store customers have access. Further the period of time for the responsive documents is limited to a period of three years prior to the date of the incident. No-post-incident responsive documents need be produced. Any responsive documents which are claimed to be privileged shall be logged on a privilege log which shall be provided to Plaintiff. Any claim of privilege for "Customer Accident Forms" (Frischer Declaration, Exh. F) is overruled as the form is completed and signed by the potential claimant and does not constitute a confidential attorney-client communication or a report prepared by an employee at the direction of counsel. Safeway may redact the names, addresses and phone numbers of customers other than Plaintiff listed on any such non-privileged responsive documents. Request No.5 seeks "[c]opies of all reports and/or complaints with regard to a condition of the [store] where the INCIDENT occurred." The request seeking relevant and probative evidence is granted but limited to non-privileged reports and/or complaints that pertain to those interior parts of the store to which store customers have access, to the extent such exist. The time period shall be limited to a period of three years prior to the date of the incident. No post-incident responsive documents need be produced. Safeway may redact the names, addresses and phone numbers of customers other than Plaintiff listed on any such non-privileged responsive documents. (continued on part 2). =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RYAN VS. SAFEWAY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 17, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Amended Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling). (part 2 of 2). Request No. 6 seeks "[c]opies of any DOCUMENTS describing or referring to any system or routine of inspections and or maintenance of the walking surfaces at the [store] that were in effect as of the date of the incident." This request seeks responsive and probative evidence. To the extent that there are any additional responsive documents beyond the "Sweep policy" already produced, they shall be produced subject to the terms of the agreed upon protective order. Request No. 10 seeks "[c]opies of all logs or daily reports or other DOCUMENTS that REFER TO the cleaning, maintenance or repairs of the [store] for the 30 days before and the 30 days after the INCIDENT." This request also seeks relevant and probative evidence, but is overbroad as to scope and time. Safeway shall be required to produce responsive non-privileged documents which pertain to the cleaning, maintenance and repair of the area of the store where Plaintiff was injured for a period of 30 days before the incident shall be produced. No post-incident responsive documents need be produced. A privilege log shall also be provided for any privileged documents. Each party's respective requests for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL S. KECK VS. KIMBERLY COLEMAN-CURRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 30, 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT ERIC SAFIRE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. First, the special demurrers (uncertainty, cannot ascertain type of contract) are improper because this is a limited jurisdiction case. (CCP 92(c)). Second, the only cause of action asserted against the moving defendant (Mr. Safire) is for common counts. The only essential allegations are (1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum, (2) the consideration, and (3) nonpayment. See Witkin, Cal. Procedure, Pleading §§ 553, 557. Here, plaintiff makes those allegations and the court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)
